title: isoetes variabilis subsp. boryana
intro: Isoetes variabilis subsp. boryana is a subspecies of the Isoetes variabilis plant, also known as the quillwort or isote, which is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Quillwort family (Isoetaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Isoetes variabilis subsp. boryana are believed to include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially treating certain respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and rhizomes can be dried and used as a tea or tincture, or combined with other herbs to create a salve or topical application.
